title: Variable Vault Vipers
short_description: The vault opens when the number in box x hits the target. Feed the viper operation pills — add, subtract, double, stash a value in M and recall it — to change x to exactly the right number.
description: A variable is just a labelled box that holds a value you can change — and in this vault, the box is called x. The lock pops open only when x equals the target, so you tap operation pills (+3, −2, ×2, and at the top an M box you can STORE into and RECALL from) to update x step by step, watching its value change as your little program runs. It teaches the core of coding: variables and state, assignment and updating, and reading the current value before your next move. Grade bands ramp from two or three simple updates, to doubling and bigger targets, to using a second memory variable M to stash and reuse a value. Cracking the vault in par or fewer moves and building streaks earns bonus points. A free online variables and state coding game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap operation pills to change the value in x. Undo and Reset to rethink.
